statement: Foreign tourists visiting Iceland totalled approximately 2.2 million in 2024. EU/EEA nationals accounted for roughly 55–60% of all visitors, with the United States being the single largest source country (~25%). Tourism revenue represented approximately 30% of total foreign exchange earnings. Iceland's participation in the Schengen Area facilitates frictionless travel from other Schengen countries.
caveats: Tourism volumes are sensitive to exchange rates, airline capacity, and global events. The US market share has grown significantly since 2010 and is not dependent on EU membership. Schengen membership is separate from EU membership (Iceland is already in Schengen via the EEA).

related_entries.statement: In 2024, Iceland's goods exports were dominated by three sectors: marine products (~39% of total goods exports), aluminium and other metals (~33%), and manufactured goods including pharmaceuticals and medical devices (~12%). Services exports, led by tourism, have grown to roughly equal goods exports in value.

citations.report_title: Bandaríkjamönnum hugnast ekki innganga Íslands í ESB

claims.claim_text: ESB-aðild Íslands gæti veikt lítillega viðskiptahagsmuni Bandaríkjanna gagnvart Íslandi.
